title: Understanding the Forces
text: Consider the forces acting on the dice. The tension in the string has two components: one vertical and one horizontal. The vertical component balances the weight of the dice, and the horizontal component is due to the car's acceleration.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: Use trigonometry to relate the angle of the string to the acceleration of the car. The tangent of the angle is the ratio of the horizontal component of the tension to the vertical component.

title: Calculate the Tangent
text: Calculate the tangent of the angle: $$tan(3.20$$ degrees).

title: Relate Tangent to Acceleration
text: The tangent of the angle is equal to the acceleration of the car divided by the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.81$$ m/s²). Set up the equation: $$tan(3.20)=\frac{a}{9.81}$$.

title: Solve for Acceleration
text: Solve the equation for the acceleration of the car: $$a=9.81tan\left(3.2\right)$$.
